prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a soft-focus close-up of a blonde woman facing slightly toward the camera. She has styled, voluminous hair, visible eye makeup, lipstick, and large gold earrings. The background is bright and indistinct, giving the shot a glowing studio look. A “Classic Commercial Archives” watermark appears in the lower-left area, with a faint archive watermark across the lower center.

By the final sampled frame, the scene has changed to a group shot of three smiling women posed closely together against the same bright, soft background. The woman on the left wears a tan cowboy hat and has long curled hair. The woman in the center has long, wavy light-brown/blonde hair and appears to wear a light-colored scarf or high-collared garment. The woman on the right has dark hair, darker skin, and wears a white or light-colored top. All three are looking toward the camera or slightly toward one another, smiling. The same archive watermarks remain visible.

The sampled frames do not show the intervening action between the opening close-up and the final group pose, so any movement, product shots, text, or transitions during that interval are not visible.
